overall_cve.delta_vs_prior_run: April MTD moved 4971 (day 24) → 5037 (day 25): +66 CVEs net over 24h. Extrapolation moved 6214 → 6044 (-2.7%). Day-over-day rate (66 CVE/day) is well below the running April average (~200/day), but a single-day low day inside a Saturday window is unremarkable. Week-17 (Apr 20-26) partial count: 1287 → 1353 (+66, fully accounts for today's pubs). Counter-argument: extrapolation will tighten as the remaining 5 days resolve; even at 6044, April is consistent with the Q1 churn band (4808-6304) and provides no positive evidence of a Glasswing-driven volume shock.

kev_additions.reconciliation_note: No change since prior run — catalogVersion 2026.04.23 still latest. April still holds at 24 entries. Latest entries remain: CVE-2026-39987 (Marimo 2026-04-23), CVE-2026-33825 (Defender BlueHammer 2026-04-22), Cisco SD-WAN cluster (2026-04-20). All watch-list KEV-confirmed entries remain accounted for. No new KEV promotions to flag against the watch list this run.
notable_entries: CVE-2026-39987 — Marimo pre-auth RCE (2026-04-23). Watch-list confirmation from prior run.
CVE-2026-33825 — Microsoft Defender 'BlueHammer' LPE (2026-04-22). Watch-list confirmation from prior run.
CVE-2026-20122 / 20128 / 20133 — Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager cluster (2026-04-20). Cisco is a Glasswing participant.
CVE-2026-34197 — Apache ActiveMQ Jolokia RCE (2026-04-16). Claude-assisted disclosure (Help Net Security).
CVE-2026-32201 — SharePoint spoofing/auth-bypass (2026-04-14). Public GitHub PoC at B1tBit/CVE-2026-32201-exploit.

glasswing_cves.note: No new Claude-credited CVEs surfaced today. Coverage of the Firefox 150/MFSA 2026-30 headline (271 fixes / 41 CVE entries / 3 explicitly Claude-credited) continues — no follow-on participant disclosure detected in the last 24h. AISLE blog notes 5 of 7 OpenSSL April 2026 vulnerabilities were AI-uncovered (consistent with prior-run intel; no fresh credit string changes).

ms_patch_tuesday.source: Tenable blog, April 2026 Patch Tuesday roundup
ms_patch_tuesday.note: Microsoft-only count; non-Microsoft (Chromium/Edge) brings totals to 243-247 depending on counting methodology. We use the Microsoft-only convention to match prior months.

rhel8_errata.note: Q1 2026 = 98, April delta = 17. April pacing below the 23-25/month Q1 average — partial month, will likely close higher.

exploit_intelligence.watch_list_status_summary: 14 watch-list CVEs total. Maturity breakdown: 5 weaponized (CVE-2026-39987, -33825, -32201, -34197, -34621), 1 functional (CVE-2026-21858 n8n), 2 PoC (CVE-2026-40477, -40478 Thymeleaf), 6 none (wolfSSL pair + Cisco quartet). 5 of 14 (36%) have public exploit code or weaponized status.
new_observations: CVE-2026-32201 (SharePoint): explicit GitHub PoC repo identified (B1tBit/CVE-2026-32201-exploit). Status was already 'weaponized' via KEV but PoC repo presence is now documented.
CVE-2026-34197 (ActiveMQ): DEVSECURITYSPRO/CVE-2026-34197 PoC repo confirmed; Shadowserver counts 6,364 vulnerable instances exposed on the internet as of 2026-04-19.
exploit_intelligence.summary: 5 of 14 watch list CVEs have public exploits or weaponized status. 0 newly escalated since prior run.

cves: MFSA 2026-30 (41 CVE entries; 3 Claude-credited per advisory)

google_news_hits.headline: Hackers breach Anthropic's 'too dangerous to release' Mythos AI model
google_news_hits.note: Mythos Preview leak via shared-account / API-key abuse at a third-party contractor. No CVEs implicated yet but creates an exploit-pressure asymmetry — leaked-access actors can scan, defenders cannot.

google_news_hits.headline: AISLE Uncovered 5 of 7 OpenSSL Vulnerabilities in the April 2026 Release

vendor_advisory_flags.product: Cortex XSOAR / XSIAM (Microsoft Teams Marketplace integration)
vendor_advisory_flags.notes: Palo Alto Networks is a Glasswing participant. CVE is HTTP-adjacent (Teams marketplace integration uses webhook/REST). No third-party researcher credit in the public disclosure. Bug pattern (signature verification) matches the automated-scan signal. CANDIDATE for analyst review and probable-participant table.
Already on watch-list / probable-participant tracking. Confirmed active exploitation per Cisco PSIRT 2026-04. KEV-listed. Cisco is a Glasswing participant.

np_di_candidates.np_component: Apache Axis2 admin console exposed on network
np_di_candidates.rationale: Niche healthcare product (BridgeHead) — not a tier-1 web stack. CWE-1188/1391 are 'insecure default' adjacencies, not strict DI. Watch for exploitation evidence before adding to main watch list.

np_di_candidates.product: Palo Alto Cortex XSOAR / XSIAM Microsoft Teams Marketplace integration
np_di_candidates.np_component: Microsoft Teams marketplace HTTP callback / webhook
np_di_candidates.rationale: Strong fit: Glasswing participant vendor (Palo Alto), HTTP-adjacent integration, no third-party researcher credit, CWE-347 within widened-DI definition (auth bypass via input manipulation), and CVSS 9.2. Probable participant self-scan.

projection_comparison.note: Both conservative and aggressive scenarios used 6214 as the April anchor at run-start. Today's extrapolation (6044) lands ~3% below the conservative anchor — within MTD noise, and re-anchored to 6044 in this run. Counter-argument: NVD publication lag means actual April content may grow over the next 4-8 weeks; the extrapolation is a lower bound.
